_format: TLS 1.2 PRF (P_SHA256) test vectors. Each vector documents its source. mbedTLS-derived vectors use the project's official test data; synthesized vectors are generated via an independent Python reference (hashlib HMAC-SHA256) and cross-checked against the mbedTLS vector to confirm the reference is correct.

prfVectors.name: mbedtls_v3_6_6_tls_prf_sha256
prfVectors.label: test tls_prf label
prfVectors._source: mbedTLS v3.6.6 tests/suites/test_suite_ssl.data — SSL TLS_PRF MBEDTLS_SSL_TLS_PRF_SHA256

prfVectors.name: short_secret_100_byte_output
prfVectors.label: expansion check
prfVectors._source: Synthesized via Python ref P_SHA256 (RFC 5246 §5). HMAC-SHA256 from hashlib; 100-byte output exercises 4 P_SHA256 blocks (each 32 B).

prfVectors.name: empty_seed_empty_label
prfVectors._source: Synthesized via Python ref P_SHA256 (RFC 5246 §5). Verifies empty label/seed don't crash.

masterSecretVector.name: master_secret_synth
masterSecretVector._source: Synthesized via Python ref P_SHA256. PMS is 32 bytes of 0xAA (mimics EcJpakePms output length). Random nonces have a single non-zero leading byte for visual separation.

keyBlockVector.name: key_block_synth_aes128_ccm_8
keyBlockVector._source: Synthesized via Python ref P_SHA256. Layout per RFC 5246 §6.3 + RFC 6655 §3 (AES-128-CCM-8 AEAD, no MAC keys): 16+16 keys then 4+4 IVs. Seed order is REVERSED vs master_secret (server then client) — see mbedTLS ssl_tls.c v3.6.6 around line 7257 ('Swap the client and server random values').

finishedVectors.name: finished_client_synth
finishedVectors._source: Synthesized via Python ref P_SHA256. transcriptDigest = SHA-256(b'dummy handshake messages'); 12-byte output per RFC 5246 §7.4.9.

finishedVectors.name: finished_server_synth
finishedVectors._source: Synthesized via Python ref P_SHA256. Same transcript as finished_client_synth, server role swaps the label to 'server finished'.
